STORE DEPARTMENT, BRANCH OFFICE, AND DEPOT.

Belvedere Road, Lambeth, S.E.

Superintendent of General Supplies and Shipments.—J. Davies, Esq.
Assistant Superintendent of General Supplies and Shipments.—Capt. C. M. Watson, R.E.
Superintendent of Military Supplies.—Lieut., Col. C. Hunter, R.A.
Supervisors, First Grade.—Gerald H. Talbot, George Arnold, George McKeon, and James Graham, Esqs.
Supervisors, Second Grade.—W. Edgell, W. H. Hooker, D. E. Sinclair, and J. Byatt, Esqs.
Second Class Clerks.—H. C. Card, T. B. Couche, W. M. Biden, H. B. Walker, S. K. Letheren, H. G. Beard, A. G. Taunton, and M. Kelly, Esqs.
Examiners of Cloth.—Messrs. C. Beard, and R. H. Buck.
Examiners of Stores.—Messrs. A. Riviere, H. Ingle, J. Clayton, W. Chambers, G. Nott, J. W. Stone, G. Garratt, T. Weeks, and J. O'Neill.
Assistant Do.—Messrs. W. McDonald, W. Weeks, W. Lunt, W. Tassell, H. G. Forbes, H. Diblin, E. J. Stone, R. May, R. W. Sadler, R. G. Barnes, G. May, G. S. Francis, R. Amsden, and O. Chapman.
Shipping Foreman and Custom House Agent.—Mr. T. Melvey.
Inspector of Scientific Instruments.—Thomas Cushing, Esq.
Examiner of Surgical Instruments.—G. D. Pollock, Esq.
Examiner of Veterinary Instruments.—Professor H. Simmonds.
Messengers.—G. Pile and T. Hodges.

STORE DEPARTMENT, STAMP BRANCH

Bunhill Row, E.C.

Superintendent.—W. H. Hosking, Esq.
Assistant Superintendents of Stamps.—Messrs. W. J. Raven and T. Gay.
Examiners of Stamps.—Messrs. G. Peters, C. Gay, G. Hobson, and W. J. Raven, jun.

HOME ESTABLISHMENT.INDIAN TROOP SERVICE vis-à-vis EGYPT.—Temporary Arrangement.

The business of the Troop Service is under the superintendence of Admiral Sir W. R. Mends, K.C.B., Director of Transport Services at the Admiralty.

Consulting Officer for the Indian Troop Service.—Captain C. J. Cruttenden, late I.N.

Transport Officer, (Suez), and Agent to the Government of India in Egypt.—Commander A. W.

Chitty, late I.N.

Clerk at Suez.—C. Turner, Esq.

Resident Transport Officer (Bombay).—Lieut. G. O'B. Carew, late I.N.

Assistant do.—Lieut H. Morland, late I.N.

Sir Richard Temple, Bart., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., D.C.L.

W. H. Barlow, Esq., F.R.S., President, Institution of Civil Engineers

Professor of Construction.—Callcott Reilly, Esq., M.I.C.E.

Professor of Mathematics.—Rev. J. Wolstenholme, M.A.

Secretary.—J. G. Whiffin, Esq., R.N., Retired.

Chaplain.—Rev. C. Crosleigh, B.D.

THE MOST EXALTED ORDER OF THE
STAR OF INDIA,†

INSTITUTED 1861. ENLARGED 1866, 1875, and 1876.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER.

The QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER OF THE ORDER.

His Excellency (THE MARQUIS OF RIPON, K.G., G.M.I.E.), the VICEROY and GOVERNOR
GENERAL of INDIA for the time being.

Knights Grand Commanders.

INDIA.

VICEROY AND GOVERNOR-GENERAL OF INDIA, The Right Hon. The MARQUIS OF RIPON, K.G., G.C.B., P.C.

Took his seat 8th June, 1880.

PERSONAL STAFF OF THE GOVERNOR-GENERAL.

Private Secretary.—H. W. Primrose

Military Secretary.—Major G. S. White, 92nd Highlanders.

Aides-de-Camp.

Capt. Lord W. L. De la Poer Beresford, V.C.

Capt. E. H. Clough-Taylor, 23rd Foot

Lieut. E. L. S. Brett, Scots Guards.

Subadar Major Nutha Sing, Sirdar Bahadur

Capt. The Hon. C. W. Fitzwilliam, Royal Horse Guards.

Extra Aides-de-Camp.

Lieut. C. W. Muir, B.S.C.

Lieut. A. W. Perry

Surgeon.

Surg. Maj. Dr. J. Anderson.

COUNCIL.

Extraordinary Member, Gen. Sir Frederick P. Haines, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Commander-in-Chief. Took his seat 15th April, 1876.

Ord. Member, Whitley Stokes, Esq., C.S.I., C.I.E. Took his seat 18th April, 1877.

Ord. Member, A. Rivers Thompson, C.S.I. Took his seat 18th April, 1878.

Ord. Member, James Gibbs, C.S.I. Took his seat 10 May 80

Ord. Member, Lieut.-Gen. Sir D. M. Stewart, G.C.B.

Ord. Member, Major Evelyn Baring, C.S.I., R.A.

Extraordinary Members, The Governors of the other presidencies, when the Council shall assemble within their territory.

Additional Members, for the purpose of making Laws and Regulations.

Sir R. E. Egerton, K.C.S.I., C.I.E.

Sayyad Ahmed Khan Bahadur, C.S.I.

B. W. Colvin.

Maharajah Jotindra Mohun Tagore, C.S.I.

Shashia Sastri, C.S.I.

J. Pitt Kennedy.

G. C. Paul, C.I.E.

H. H. Ragbir Sing, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Rajah of Jheend.

H. J. Reynolds

Secretary to Council.—D. Fitzpatrick.

SECRETARIAT.HOME REVENUE AND AGRICULTURAL DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, C. E. Bernard, C.S.I.

Under Secs., F. C. Daukes and C. J. Lyall

Registrar, J. A. Stowell

Clerks, 1st Class, W. J. Thorpe, Promotho Gangooly, W. L. Francis, and C. H. Sampson

FINANCE AND COMMERCE DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, R. B. Chapman, C.S.I.

Under Sec., E. J. Sinkinson

Registrar, W. C. Fink

Clerk, 1st Class, H. C. Dias

FOREIGN DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, A. C. Lyall, C.B.

Assist. Sec., H. M. Durand

Under Sec., T. J. C. Plowden.

Registrar, R. H. Cooke

Senior Attaché, Lieut. H. L. Ramsay

Attaché, Kazee Syud Ahmed

Assistant, 1st Class, Deputy-Registrar C. Stuart

2nd Class, A. Bourihon, and C. H. Macleod

3rd Class, M. Belletty, and I. C. Martin

LEGISLATIVE DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, D. Fitzpatrick

Deputy Sec., J. M. Macpherson.

Asst. Sec., Major H. S. Jarrett

MILITARY DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, Col. G. Chesney, R.E.

Deputy Sec., Col. W. M. Lees, B.S.C.

Asst. Secs., 1st, Capt. E. H. H. Collen; 2nd, Major A. C. W. Crookshank; 3rd, Capt. T. Deane, B.S.C. Capt. H. A. Sawyer, B.S.C., (Marine), Commander, A. J. Street, R.N.

Addl. Asst. Sec.—Major G. L. K. Hewett, B.S.C.

Accountant Gen. Major O. R. Newmarch, S.C.

Asst. Acct. Gen. Hon. Capt. J. Robertson

PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT.

Secretary, Maj. Genl. A. Fraser, R.E., C.B.

Deputy Secs., Maj. Gen. J. Crofton, R.E., Lieut.

Col. J. J. McLeod Innes, R.E., V.C.

Asst. Sec., Capt. G. F. L. Marshall, R.E.

Under Sec., A. B. Sampson

LAW DEPARTMENT.HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE.

ESTABLISHED 1862.

Chief Justice—Hon. Sir R. Garth, Kt., Q.C.

Judges—Charles Pontifex, G. G. Morris, J. Sewell White, Romesh Chunder Mitter, B.L., H. S. Cunningham, W. F. McDonell, V.C., Arthur Wilson, H. T. Prinsep, L. R. Tottenham, L. P. D. Broughton, A. T. Maclean, C. D. Field.

PUBLIC WORKS DEPARTMENT.SECRETARIAT.

Secretary—Major-Gen. Alexander Fraser, C.B., R.E.

CIVIL AND MILITARY WORKS BRANCH (INCLUDING IRRIGATION AND TELEGRAPH).

Inspector General of Irrigation and Deputy Secretary—Maj.-Gen. J. Crofton, R.E.

Assistant Secretary—Capt. G. F. L. Marshall, R.E.

RAILWAY BRANCH.

Deputy Secretary—Lt. Col. E. C. S. Williams, C.I.E.—on leave

Under Secretary—Captain F. Firebrace, R.E.

ACCOUNT BRANCH.

Accountant General and Deputy Secretary—Col. J. J. McLeod Innes, R.E., V.C.—on furl.

Deputy Accountant General and ex-officio Under Secretary—Major A. J. Filgate, R.E.

Assistant Accountant General—R. G. Macdonald.

ESTABLISHMENT BRANCH.

Under Secretary—A. B. Sampson.

Consulting Engineer to Government of India for State Railways—G. L. Molesworth, C.I.E.

Assistant to Ditto.—E. H. Stone.

MEMO.—The Superintendents of the Central Jails (except Allahabad) are also Superintendents of the District Jails at the same stations. The remaining District Jails are usually in charge of the Civil Surgeons of the several Districts.

GRADATION LIST OF GENERAL AND FIELD OFFICERS OF
THE INDIAN ARMY.

B. Bengal; M. Madras; Bo. Bombay; C. Cavalry; I. Infantry; S.C. Staff Corps.

NOTE.—The names, in Italics, of Officers of the Staff Corps (who are borne for promotion on a separate List) are retained upon this list for the purpose of regulating the promotion of Officers of the Indian Army, —See Page 197.

BRITISH CAVALRY.13th Hussars.

"Fare in aeternum."

"Peninsula"—"Waterloo"—"Alma"—"Balaklava"—"Inkerman"—"Sevastopol."

Blue. Buff Collars—B. b., Buff—Pl., White.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 13th February, 1874. Kandahar.

Depôt at Canterbury.

BRITISH INFANTRY.2nd (Queen's Royal).

SECOND BATTALION.

The Paschal Lamb.

"Pristinæ virtutis memor"—"Vel exuviae triumphant."

The Royal Cypher within the Garter.

"Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—"Vimiera"—"Corunna"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Pyrenees"
"Nivells"—"Toulouse"—"Peninsula"—"Afghanistan"—"Ghuzee"
"Khelat"—"Taku Forts"—"Pekin."

Landed at Bombay from Malta, 1st March, 1878.—Peshawar.

Brigade Depôt,—Guildford, Sub-District No. 48.

BRITISH INFANTRY.5th (Northumberland Fusiliers).SECOND BATTALION.

"Quo Fata Vocant"—St. George an the Dragon—The Rose and Crown—The King's Crest—"Wilhelmstah"—
"Roleia"—"Vimiera"—"Coronna"—"Busaco"—"Ciudad Rodrigo"—"Badajoz"—"Salamanca"—
"Vittoria"—"Nivelle"—"Orthes"—"Toulouse"—"Peninsula"—"Lucknow"

Scarlet—Facings Bright Green.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 3rd February, 1880. Agra.

Brigade Depôt,—Berwick-on-Tweed. Sub-District No. 1.

BRITISH INFANTRY.

6th (Royal First Warwickshire).

SECOND BATTALION.

The Antelope—The Rose and Crown—"Ro'eta"—"Vinierra"—"Coronna"—"Vittoria"—"Pyrennes"—
"Nivelle"—"Orthes"—"Peninsula"—"Niagara."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived in India, 26th October, 1878.—Cawnpore.

Brigade Depôt,—Warwick, Sub-District No. 28.

BRITISH INFANTRY.

8th (King's).

2ND BATTALION.

"Nec aspera terrent."

The White Horse within the Garter—The King's Cypher and Crown—"Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—
"Marthique"—"Niagara"—"Delhi"—"Lucknow."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived at Bombay Oct. 1877. Meean Meer.

Brigade Depôt,—Warrington, Sub-District No. 13.

BRITISH INFANTRY.
9th (East Norfolk).

2ND BATTALION.

The Figure of Britannia—

Roleia"—"Viineira"—"Corunna"—"Busaco"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Saint Sebastian"—"Nivo"—
"Peninsula"—"Cabool," 1842—"Moodkee"—"Ferozeshah"—"Sobraon"—"Sevastopol."

Scarlet—Facings Yellow.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 26th November 1874. Subathu.

Brigade Depot, Yarmouth, Sub-District No. 31.

12th (East Suffolk).

1ST BATTALION.Minden "—Gibraltar" (with the Castle, Key and motto Montis Insignia Calpe)—"Seringapatam,"—"India,"—"New Zealand."

The Harp and Crown. | The Lion of Nassau.

12th (Prince of Wales' Royal) Lancers.

The Plume of the Prince of Wales, the Rising Sun and the Red Dragon. "Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—
"Peninsula"—"Waterloo"—"Sevastopol"—"Central India."

Blue—Facings and Plume Scarlet.

Arrived in India on the 31st January, 1877. Secunderabad.—Arrived on the 5th February, 1877,
from England.

Blue—Busby-Bag, yellow; Plume, white.

Arrived in India on the 7th February, 1876. Bangalore.—Arrived from England 14 Feb., 1876.

"Queen's Own" Sappers and Miners.

Seringapatam"—"Java"—"Egypt"—"Assaye"—"Mahidpore"—"Nagpore"—
"Ava"—"Lucknow"—"Central India."

A, C, and E Companies, "Pegu."

A Company bears on its appointments "Taku Forts," and "Pekin."

A, B, and F Companies bear on their appointments, a Dragon, wearing an imperial Crown, with the word "China."

B Company bears on its appointments "Pegu" and "Persia."

C Company bears on its appointments "Meeanee" and "Hyderabad, 1843."

G, H, and K Companies "Abyssinia."

K Company "Taku Forts."

BOMBAY.

GOVERNOR.

The Right Honourable Sir JAMES FERGUSON, Bart., K.C.M.G.

Took his seat, 28 Apr. 1880.

Name and Rank. Corps. Date. Remarks.

INDEX.—ACTIVE SERVICE.

NOTE.—Native names will be found under the first portion of the name, omitting the following words when used as titles:—Babu, Bakshi, Haji, Jemadar, Kazi, Khan Bahadur, Khan Sahib, Khwajah, Maharajah, Maulvi, Mir, Mirza, Nawab, Pundit, Rai, Rai Bahadur, Rajah, Rao, Rao Bahadur, Rao Saheb, Seth, Shahzadah, Shaikh, Sirdar, Subadar, Syud, and Wazirzadah.
